global.title: US continues Iran campaign as Houthis announce Saudi blockade - Reuters
global.summary: The U.S. says it has completed a 10th straight night of strikes on Iran, while Houthi forces announced a blockade on Saudi Arabia in the Bab el-Mandeb Strait, raising the risk of wider regional and energy disruption.
global.whyItMatters: This deepens instability in the Middle East and may shape prayers, pastoral care, and preaching on peace, fear, and global suffering.

global.title: Market Talk: 'Severe oil supply shortages' possible if Houthis block Red Sea - Reuters
global.summary: Reuters highlights warnings that a Houthi blockade in the Red Sea could trigger serious oil supply shortages, adding an economic dimension to the expanding regional conflict.
global.whyItMatters: Pastors should watch how war can quickly affect household anxiety, costs, and congregational needs far from the battlefield.

global.title: Japanese mayor marks national first by taking maternity leave - Reuters
global.summary: A Japanese mayor is drawing national attention by becoming the first in the country to take maternity leave, signaling a notable cultural shift around family and public leadership.
global.whyItMatters: This offers a timely illustration of leadership that honors family life, care, and humane rhythms of work.

canadian.title: Ontario wildfires expand as smoke lingers in parts of the US - Reuters
canadian.summary: Ontario says wildfires have grown to 735,000 hectares, with 190 active blazes and about 1,800 evacuees in northwestern communities. Air quality has improved in much of southern Ontario but remains unhealthy in parts of the U.S. Midwest.
canadian.whyItMatters: This remains a major Canadian crisis with direct implications for prayer, generosity, displacement, and creation care.

canadian.title: Earthquake of magnitude 5.6 strikes central Peru - Reuters
canadian.summary: Within Reuters' Canada-focused video lineup, Prime Minister Carney says Canada will not share Gordie Howe bridge tolls with the U.S. until the debt is repaid, underscoring fresh cross-border tension.
canadian.whyItMatters: Canada-U.S. friction can affect public mood, economic confidence, and how pastors help people process national uncertainty without rancor.

persecutedChurch.title: Traditionalist Catholics face a difficult choice after Vatican cuts off breakaway group - AP News
persecutedChurch.summary: After Vatican action against a breakaway traditionalist Catholic group, many worshippers are facing a crisis of conscience over church unity, authority, and where they belong spiritually.
persecutedChurch.whyItMatters: Church division, conscience, and belonging are pastoral issues that resonate beyond Catholic contexts and can inform teaching on unity and humility.

persecutedChurch.title: A pastor’s lengthy ICE detention leaves his Colorado parishioners torn - The Washington Post
persecutedChurch.summary: A 78-year-old Anglican pastor remains in ICE detention, and his congregation is struggling to reconcile support for immigration enforcement with compassion for a shepherd they know personally.
persecutedChurch.whyItMatters: This is a vivid pastoral case study in how policy debates become deeply human inside a church community.

leadership.title: Why Traditional Change Leadership Isn’t Enough When Change Never Stops - Forbes
leadership.summary: Forbes argues that constant change requires more than strategy; leaders need self-awareness, trust-building, emotional regulation, and healthy accountability to guide people well.
leadership.whyItMatters: Useful counsel for leading a church through ongoing uncertainty without losing clarity, culture, or compassion.

goodNews.title: Singapore firms lauded for doing good | The Straits Times
goodNews.summary: A total of 481 firms were recognized for community impact, with businesses donating nearly $384 million over the past three years.
goodNews.whyItMatters: It is an encouraging reminder that generosity and public good can still scale, offering examples worth celebrating from the pulpit or in leadership conversations.
